vulnerability.reference: Research communities prioritizing model scale over stability, propagating flawed architectural modifications.
vulnerability.generated: Deep stacked UNet architectures propagate small numerical errors through layers without correction, causing denoising instability during training.

impact.reference: Stalled progress in large-scale generative modeling due to unreliable training outcomes.
impact.generated: Training of massively deep generative models fails, producing low-quality images and wasting computational resources, hindering research progress.

dose_response.reference: Skip-tuning introduces numerical propagation errors that degrade denoising, which in deeper stacked networks accumulates to hinder scalable training, leading to stalled progress and resource waste in generative modeling research.
dose_response.generated: Skip-tuning of shortcut connections, through researchers training massively deep vision generation models, triggers numerical error propagation in deep stacked UNet architectures, causing denoising instability that leads to training failures, low-quality images, wasted resources, and hindered progress.
